schema_version.description: 1.1.0 adds the optional control_surfaces[].surface binding field (1.0.0 documents bind by name); 1.2.0 adds the optional body block; 1.3.0 adds propulsion_bemt.n_blades; 1.4.0 adds control_surfaces[].deflection_limits_deg; 1.5.0 adds planform.placement, REQUIRED from 1.5.0 onward; 1.6.0 adds moment_reference_point, REQUIRED from 1.6.0 onward; 1.7.0 adds propulsion_bemt.airfoil_sections and propulsion_bemt.rotation_axis, both REQUIRED within propulsion_bemt from 1.7.0 onward if propulsion_bemt is present (see the version-conditional constraints below); 1.8.0 adds the duct block (EDF shroud geometry + explicit placement), REQUIRED from 1.8.0 onward -- each is optional only for documents predating its own introduction

design_id.description: Hash or UUID tying this parameter set to a specific notebook run for adjoint-loop traceability

reference_frame.description: Must match Aetherion/BEMT convention to avoid an undifferentiated transform seam

moment_reference_point.description: The vehicle CG (1.6.0) in the declared reference_frame -- the moment reference for any Cm/Cl/Cn a VLM/BEMT sweep of this geometry produces. Same convention as `body`/`planform.placement` (x = -station, 0 at the nose tip) and the SAME source `cfd/vehicle/Allrun.case`'s CofR uses (out/fuselage.yaml x_CG_m), so aero moments computed from this contract stay directly comparable to the project's CFD force/moment coefficients. Required from schema_version 1.6.0 onward (see the top-level version-conditional constraint); without it Cm has no stated centre and defaults to the coordinate-system origin, which is not a physically meaningful reference for a body whose CG sits well aft of the nose.

placement.description: Absolute position (1.5.0) of the eta=0 station's leading edge in the declared reference_frame -- the anchor every station's chord-relative offset (chord, twist, sweep_qc, dihedral) is measured from. Without it the planform has no stated relationship to `body`: a consumer that assumes the root sits at the reference-frame origin places the wing at the fuselage nose, an unphysical overlap with the body of revolution. Required from schema_version 1.5.0 onward (see the top-level version-conditional constraint); optional here only so pre-placement 1.0.0-1.4.0 documents remain structurally valid -- absence on any 1.5.0+ document is malformed, not a zero default.

stations.description: Fixed-length array across the whole design space — count must never change between runs

airfoil_sections.description: Must align 1:1 with planform.stations by eta — same count, fixed

surface.description: Lattice binding (1.1.0): the geometric surface this entry belongs to. wing: eta is the semispan fraction of the planform. duct_jet: an all-moving plate in the duct jet, eta is the duct-exit radius fraction -- never bound to a lifting-surface lattice. When absent (1.0.0), bind by name: aileron/rudder -> wing, vane -> duct_jet.

deflection_limits_deg.description: Mechanical rotation range about hinge_axis, right-hand rule, degrees (1.4.0). The accurate sweep range for CFD/VLM deflection studies -- the physical actuator/plate cannot exceed [min, max].

deflection_soft_limit_deg.description: Optional aerodynamic onset inside the mechanical range (e.g. flat-plate stall) -- informational for sweep planning, not a hard bound.

body.description: Optional (1.2.0): the fuselage as a body of revolution in the declared reference frame -- x = 0 at the nose tip, negative aft, stations span [-length, 0], nose -> tail. Fixed station count across the design space (remeshing is an outer-loop event). radius is 0 at the nose tip; the aft-most station carries the tail-base (hub) radius. Not bound to any lifting lattice -- intended for slender-body/Munk trim corrections and duct-jet context.

duct.description: The EDF shroud (1.8.0): an annulus of revolution about the body x-axis through placement.center, the SAME parameterisation the CAD duct solid is built from (out/fuselage.yaml D_duct_inner_m / D_duct_outer_m / duct_chord_m and the layout duct station). inner_diameter is the bore and carries the rotor tip clearance (inner_diameter/2 > propulsion_bemt.disk_radius, exporter-guarded); chord is the axial length. placement.center is the mid-chord point on the duct centreline in the declared reference_frame, same x = -station convention as `body`: the inlet (forward) face sits at x = center.x + chord/2, the exit face at x = center.x - chord/2. The duct legitimately overhangs the body's tail base (the jet vanes work in its exit flow) -- do not clamp it to the body extent. Required from schema_version 1.8.0 onward (see the top-level version-conditional constraint): without it a duct-aware consumer (BEMT shroud/tip-loss modelling, the duct_jet control surfaces whose eta is measured on the duct-EXIT radius) has no stated duct geometry at all and no way to place the prop-in-duct against `body`.

n_blades.description: Blade count (1.3.0). Required for solidity/thrust in BEMT; blade_stations alone describes one blade's chord/twist law, not the rotor.

airfoil_sections.description: Blade 2D section shape per radial station (1.7.0), aligned 1:1 with blade_stations by r_over_R -- a real, well-documented low-Re propeller airfoil (Clark Y; see config/airfoils/clarky.dat), thickness-scaled root->tip, NOT a proprietary/parametric shape. Same CST representation as the top-level airfoil_sections (wing).

rotation_axis.description: Angular velocity vector direction (1.7.0), right-hand rule, unit vector in the declared reference_frame -- same idiom as control_surfaces[].hinge_axis. Numerically derived (not assumed) from the blade twist law: the sign given here is the ONLY one consistent with the twist producing forward thrust and a positive angle of attack at every radius; the opposite sign gives ~180 deg AoA everywhere. Do not default this to a guessed sign -- BEMT thrust/torque direction and swirl sense depend on it.

mesh_topology.description: Static discretization directives — NOT design variables, must stay constant across the entire optimization to preserve gradient continuity

allOf.description: planform.placement became mandatory at 1.5.0: every schema_version NOT in the pre-placement list below must supply the wing/body anchor.
moment_reference_point became mandatory at 1.6.0: every schema_version NOT in the pre-1.6.0 list below must supply the CG/Cm reference.

propulsion_bemt.airfoil_sections and .rotation_axis became mandatory (WITHIN propulsion_bemt, if present) at 1.7.0: every schema_version NOT in the pre-1.7.0 list below must supply them when propulsion_bemt is included. propulsion_bemt itself remains entirely optional at every version -- this only constrains its contents when a document chooses to include it.
duct became mandatory at 1.8.0: every schema_version NOT in the pre-1.8.0 list below must supply the shroud geometry and its placement.
